“Golden Boy is an unassuming by-the-slice pizza place in North Beach, perfect for late-night beer-ballasting. The decor brings to mind one of those 1950s mobile homes turned inside out: long and narrow, stainless steel all over… In we go, past a couple of Haight babies talking tongue studs, a rockabilly dude moaning over his Bettie Page… The counterman appears with two thick slices steaming on the end of his spatula. Same basket? he asks… Knives and forks in red plastic baskets, cutting the crust into bites… The pizza’s crispy and chewy and delicious. She feeds herself a pepperoni off her slice, looks at me and then sucks the tips of her fingers. That’s amore.”
